channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Com fim de janela partidária, PSD de Kassab passa a ter 3ª bancada na Alesp; PSDB cai para 10ª
item.description: Following the end of the party-switching window in São Paulo’s Legislative Assembly (Alesp), Gilberto Kassab’s PSD grew from 4 to 11 deputies, becoming the third-largest caucus, while the PSDB dropped from 8 to 2 deputies, falling from fourth to tenth place. PL and Republicanos each gained two deputies, strengthening Tarcísio de Freitas’ base, whereas Cidadania, PDT, and Rede lost all representation. Most deputies (82%) plan to run for re-election in the October state elections.

item.title: Tarcísio autoriza construção de centro para PcD vítimas de violência em São Paulo
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas authorized the construction of a center in São Paulo city to provide integrated care for people with disabilities who are victims of violence. The facility will offer multidisciplinary support, including health, social assistance, and rights protection.

item.title: Cotado para disputar o governo de Minas Gerais, senador Rodrigo Pacheco se filia ao PSB
item.description: Senator Rodrigo Pacheco (PSB-MG) officially joined the Brazilian Socialist Party (PSB) in Brasília, highlighting the party's long history and commitment to fighting authoritarianism. The move aligns him with President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's strategy to support Lula's re-election bid in Minas Gerais, a key electoral state. Pacheco is being considered as a potential candidate for governor of Minas Gerais in 2026 to strengthen federal-state alliances.

item.title: ‘Vocês são as pessoas honestas que querem que eu seja’, diz Lula, em ato falho, no Ceará
item.description: President Lula made a verbal slip while speaking to youth in Ceará, telling them they 'are the honest people I want to be,' amid a discussion about young people's belief that all politicians are corrupt. He urged them not to give up on politics, emphasizing that everyone can take responsibility, despite widespread skepticism.

item.title: Governo federal faz mais duas trocas em ministérios antes das eleições
item.description: Environment Minister Marina Silva and Transport Minister Renan Filho formally resigned from their federal cabinet positions on Wednesday to run in the October elections, with Silva seeking a Senate seat in São Paulo and Filho aiming for the governorship of Alagoas. They are being replaced by their respective executive secretaries, João Paulo Capobianco and George Palermo Santoro. Approximately 18 of President Lula’s 37 ministers are expected to step down to run for office, complying with Brazil’s electoral incompatibility rule, which requires public officials to leave their posts up to six months before elections — a deadline that falls on April 4.

item.title: Lindbergh aciona STF e Conselho de Ética contra Alfredo Gaspar
item.description: Federal Deputy Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ) filed a criminal complaint with the STF and an ethics complaint with the Chamber’s Council of Ethics against colleague Alfredo Gaspar (PL-AL) for crimes against honor and breach of parliamentary decorum, following Gaspar’s unsubstantiated accusation during the INSS CPMI that Lindbergh had raped a minor. Lindbergh states he acted as a citizen and congressman by forwarding information received from journalists through legal and institutional channels.

item.title: Comitê dos EUA critica Moraes e mira eleições de 2026
item.description: The U.S. House Judiciary Committee released a report criticizing Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes for allegedly restricting freedom of expression in Brazil. Titled 'The Attack on Free Speech Abroad: The Case of Brazil,' it is the third in a series examining judicial overreach. The report implies that such actions could affect the political climate ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Leite e Caiado conversam por telefone após ruído no PSD
item.description: Eduardo Leite, governor of Rio Grande do Sul, called Ronaldo Caiado on Tuesday night, the 31st, amid internal PSD tension following Leite's public remarks against Caiado's presidential pre-candidacy. The call came days after the PSD formally named Caiado as its presidential pre-candidate, indicating efforts to ease party discord. The contact suggests a dialogue attempt despite recent disagreements.

item.title: Em busca de delação, advogado de Zettel se reúne com Mendonça
item.description: Fabiano Zettel, brother-in-law of banker Daniel Vorcaro and detained in Operation Compliance Zero over suspected fraud at Banco Master, is seeking a plea bargain deal. His new defense team, led by lawyer Celso Vilardi — who previously defended Jair Bolsonaro and worked on cases like Lava Jato and Mensalão — met with Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça to negotiate cooperation. The change in legal counsel followed disagreements over a plea deal strategy, given Zettel’s close ties to Vorcaro, his role as a major donor to Bolsonaro-aligned campaigns, and his potential to reveal key details about the alleged scheme involving false credit instruments and money laundering.

item.title: Relator da PEC da Segurança, Mendonça Filho deixa União Brasil após mais de 40 anos e se filia ao PL
item.description: Mendonça Filho, rapporteur of the Security PEC, announced his departure from União Brasil after over 40 years of affiliation to join the PL. The decision was driven by disagreements over the party's federation with PP and an invitation from Senators Flávio Bolsonaro and Rogério Marinho. He stated he will serve as an opposition voice to the PT in Pernambuco and defend democratic institutions.

item.title: O anúncio de Trump nesta noite sobre a guerra no Irã
item.description: Donald Trump will deliver an operational update on the Iran War tonight, after claiming Tehran proposed a ceasefire, which Iran’s foreign minister denied. President Lula criticized the war as unnecessary and denied Iran has nuclear weapons, while pushing for diesel subsidies to curb fuel prices, though states rejected cutting ICMS. Senator Rodrigo Pacheco will join the PSB to run for governor of Minas Gerais, backed by Lula and Alckmin, following months of talks with other parties.

item.title: Vorcaro, três parlamentares e dois ex-ministros viajaram juntos em jatinho de Brasília para São Paulo, mostram documentos
item.description: Documents reveal that Banco Master owner Daniel Vorcaro traveled on a private jet from Brasília to São Paulo on August 28 of last year, accompanied by Senator Ciro Nogueira (PP-PI), deputies Isnaldo Bulhões (MDB-AL) and Rodrigo Gambale (Podemos-SP), and former ministers Fábio Faria and Bruno Bianco. The trip occurred as Vorcaro sought to sell Master Bank to BRB, a deal blocked by the Central Bank six days later, leading to the bank’s liquidation in November. The records highlight Vorcaro’s political connections, which are expected to be part of his plea bargain negotiations with the PF and PGR.

item.title: O principal motivo de rejeição a Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: The Atlas/Arko poll revealed that the main reason for rejection of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), pre-candidate for President of the Republic, is the fear of a government similar to that of former President Jair Bolsonaro (PL), cited by 74.4% of respondents. Secondly, 62.7% mentioned alleged involvement in corruption as a reason for rejection. Flávio Bolsonaro is the second politician with the highest rejection rate in the survey.

item.title: Corrupção eleva rejeição a Lula, e Bolsonaro afasta eleitores de Flávio, diz Atlas
item.description: The perception of involvement or complicity in corruption is the main reason for rejection of President Lula among 85.9% of his detractors, while disapproval of Jair Bolsonaro’s government leads 74.4% of voters to reject Flávio Bolsonaro. The Atlas Intel and Arko Advice study shows 43% view Lula negatively and 44% view Flávio negatively, with corruption, authoritarianism, and opportunism being the top drivers of aversion.

item.title: Comissão do Congresso americano acusa Moraes de censura e diz que ministro pode afetar eleições
item.description: A preliminary report from the U.S. House Judiciary Committee accused Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes of imposing censorship against the Bolsonaro family and their supporters, potentially undermining the fairness of Brazil’s 2026 presidential elections. The report notes that similar censorship orders were used against negative content about Lula in the 2022 election, which was decided by a narrow margin. The committee, dominated by Trump allies, argues these actions restrict online speech in the months leading up to the vote.

item.title: Messias inicia 2º beija-mão e vê 'margem segura' para aprovação no Senado
item.description: Jorge Messias, the Attorney General of the Union nominated by President Lula for a Supreme Court seat, resumed the Senate's 'beija-mão' courting process after submitting his documentation, aiming to secure approval for his confirmation hearing. He expressed confidence in a 'safe margin' for Senate support.

item.title: O convite que Eduardo Leite recusou depois de ser preterido pelo PSD
item.description: Eduardo Leite, governor of Rio Grande do Sul, was passed over by the PSD in the presidential candidacy selection, which went to Ronaldo Caiado. He was then invited by the state MDB directory to join the party of his vice governor, Gabriel Souza, whom Leite supports as his successor. The article is subscriber-only and requires registration for full access.

item.title: Crusoé: Lula é o político mais rejeitado, aponta pesquisa
item.description: The AtlasIntel/Arko poll shows that President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) is the most rejected political figure among Brazilian voters for the 2026 elections, with 50.6% rejection. Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) follows with 24%, and former President Jair Bolsonaro (PL), despite being imprisoned and ineligible, registers 16.3% rejection. Other mentioned figures include Deputy Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG) and former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro (PL), with lower rejection rates.

item.title: TCU investiga possível uso indevido da máquina pública e desvio de finalidade em desfile na Sapucaí que homenageou Lula
item.description: The TCU is investigating alleged misuse of public resources and diversion of purpose in the Acadêmicos de Niterói samba parade honoring President Lula, following a complaint by Novo party legislators. Minister Augusto Nardes requested details on deployed personnel, costs, and legal justification for using presidential ceremonial staff in event logistics. First Lady Janja da Silva did not participate in the parade despite being slated as a highlight on the 'Friends of Lula' float.

item.title: PT x PL: a preferência partidária dos eleitores, segundo nova pesquisa Atlas
item.description: PT and PL are technically tied in party preference for the 2026 elections, with 25.4% and 27% respectively, according to an AtlasIntel/Arko Advice poll. PSOL ranks third at 8.3%, followed by Missão (MBL's acronym) at 5.9%. The survey interviewed 4,224 voters between March 16 and 23, with a two-point margin of error.

item.title: Mortes causadas por PMs aumentaram 35% em todo o estado de SP
item.description: The number of deaths caused by military police officers on duty in São Paulo state rose 35.5% in the first bimester of 2025 compared to the same period in 2024, increasing from 76 to 103 victims. This increase is part of an upward trend since 2023, with significant jumps in 2024 (83%) and 2025, reversing the decline observed between 2019 and 2022. Human rights advocates and experts attribute this escalation to the administration of Governor Tarcísio de Freitas, citing inaction on police violence and opposition to body cameras.

item.title: MEC anuncia plataformas gratuitas de idiomas e livros
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and Education Minister Camilo Santana announced two new free MEC apps: MEC Idiomas, offering free English and Spanish courses from basic to advanced, and MEC Livros, providing a digital library of 8,000 titles via gov.br, both featuring free access and 14-day renewable digital loans.

item.title: A devassa do TCU nos gastos da Presidência com o carnaval de Lula e Janja
item.description: The TCU has ordered the Presidency’s Civil House to provide, within 15 days, detailed data on government officials dispatched to Rio de Janeiro between February 1 and 18, 2026, to support Lula and Janja during the Acadêmicos de Niterói samba parade at the Sapucaí, including costs for travel, lodging, per diems, and overtime, as well as clarification on whether ceremonial staff were used for tasks like sending invitations and collecting body measurements for the 'Friends of Lula' float. The request aims to support opposition-led investigations into potential misuse of public funds.

item.title: Direita, esquerda, centro: como se divide o eleitorado a 6 meses da eleição, segundo AtlasIntel
item.description: According to an AtlasIntel/Arko Advice poll released on April 1, 2026, 36.6% of Brazilian voters identify as right-wing, 25.9% as left-wing, 17% as center-left, 8.5% as center-right, 3.3% as center, and 8.7% say they do not know or have no ideology. The survey interviewed 4,224 voters online between March 16 and 23, with a margin of error of two percentage points and a 95% confidence level.

item.title: Vídeo: Saiba quem é Jorge Messias, indicado por Lula para o cargo de ministro do STF
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has nominated Jorge Messias, a lawyer and former Attorney General of the Union, to fill a vacancy on the Supreme Federal Court (STF). The nomination was formalized with the submission of the presidential message to the Senate, where it is now undergoing the confirmation process. Messias is known for his work defending state interests in high-impact cases, particularly in matters of administrative impropriety and protection of public funds.

item.title: O sentimento de Alcolumbre com formalização de escolha de Messias para o STF
item.description: Senate President Davi Alcolumbre was displeased to learn through the press that the presidential message formally nominating Jorge Messias to the STF had been sent to Congress without prior notice. Allies say he views this as a political miscalculation by President Lula and may delay the nomination’s progress in the Senate’s CCJ committee. Despite the resentment, the nomination is expected to be approved, as inter-branch relations have improved since late last year.

item.title: Lula diz a jovens desconfiados de políticos: ‘Vocês são as pessoas honestas que querem que eu seja’
item.description: President Lula urged Brazilian youth to enter politics, arguing that widespread distrust in politicians should motivate them to participate rather than withdraw. Speaking at the two-year anniversary event of the Pé-de-Meia program at the new ITA campus in Fortaleza, he emphasized education’s role in combating online misinformation and praised Education Minister Camilo Santana and Ceará Governor Elmano de Freitas as honest public servants. Lula concluded by telling youth: 'You are the honest people you want me to be'.

item.title: Guerra contra o Irã é desnecessária e baseada em mentira, diz Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized the war by the United States and Israel against Iran, calling it unnecessary and based on false claims about Iran developing nuclear weapons. Lula recalled that in 2010, during his second term, he negotiated an agreement with Iran for peaceful uranium enrichment, similar to Brazil’s constitutional framework, but it was rejected by the U.S. and the European Union. He also expressed concern over rising diesel prices in Brazil, noting ongoing monitoring by federal police and state consumer protection agencies to prevent abusive increases.

item.title: McDonald’s lança linha de sanduíches da Copa
item.description: McDonald's launched a World Cup 2026-themed sandwich line, with pre-sales of the Brazil Combo starting April 1, 2026, and running through April 6. Beginning April 7, the full menu will be available nationwide in Brazil, featuring sandwiches from eight countries and two McCafé beverages representing Canada, one of the tournament's host nations.

item.title: Veja os próximos passos após Lula indicar Jorge Messias ao STF
item.description: Lula nominated Jorge Messias to the STF following the retirement of Luís Roberto Barroso. The appointment requires Senate approval: first in the CCJ with a hearing and secret vote, then in the plenary, where an absolute majority (41 votes) is needed. Despite prior resistance, current political conditions favor confirmation.

item.title: Chega ao Senado mensagem presidencial indicando Jorge Messias para o STF
item.description: President Lula nominated Jorge Messias for the Supreme Federal Court (STF), replacing Luís Roberto Barroso, and the nomination was sent to the Senate, where it must undergo a hearing in the Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ) and be approved by secret ballot with an absolute majority (41 senators). Messias, 45, has been Attorney General of the Union since 2023, with a career in public law and roles in the Dilma Rousseff government. Since 1894, no STF nominee has been rejected by the Senate.

item.title: Mendonça Filho deixa União Brasil após 40 anos para se filiar ao PL
item.description: Federal Deputy Mendonça Filho (PE) left União Brasil after over 40 years of affiliation to join the PL, the party of former President Jair Bolsonaro. His departure follows the formalization of the federation between União Brasil and PP, a move he had been considering since 2022. Mendonça Filho served as Minister of Education in the Temer government (2016-2018).

item.title: Após críticas à escolha de Caiado, Leite faz ligação e se coloca à disposição para ajudar na pré-campanha
item.description: After publicly criticizing the PSD's choice of Ronaldo Caiado as its presidential candidate, Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite called Caiado to clarify that his concern was with the party's internal process, not with Caiado personally, and offered to support the pre-campaign. Leite denied plans to leave the PSD, reiterated his commitment to finish his term, and emphasized efforts to position his vice-governor, Gabriel Souza, as his successor.

item.title: Valdemar Costa Neto elogia Messias e diz que Senado aprova indicação
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, president of the PL, praised the nomination of Jorge Messias to the STF, stating that although he is aligned with the PT, he is a competent choice and that the Senate will approve it due to the government majority. He said he will not influence PL senators, whose majority opposes the nomination, but acknowledged approval is certain. The formal nomination followed bureaucratic delays and resistance from Senate President Davi Alcolumbre, who preferred Rodrigo Pacheco for the vacancy.

item.title: PL dá dois meses para empresário viabilizar candidatura ao governo de MG
item.description: The PL gave two months to Fiemg president Flávio Roscoe to viable his candidacy for governor of Minas Gerais after his affiliation with the party on March 31. The goal is for him to travel across the state, increase his visibility, and demonstrate his capacity to run in the election, amid party concerns over Flávio Bolsonaro's presidential ticket in the country's second-largest electoral college.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro perdido no mundo
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro made his international debut at CPAC in Dallas, promoting a conservative agenda that frames the world as a battle between conservatism and 'globalism,' which he blames for undermining traditional family values. He positioned himself as the political heir of his father, Jair Bolsonaro, echoing rhetoric previously advanced in 2019 by former Foreign Minister Ernesto Araújo, whose foreign policy was widely ridiculed.

item.title: André de Paula toma posse do Ministério da Agricultura
item.description: André de Paula (PSD) was sworn in as Minister of Agriculture on April 1, 2026, replacing Carlos Fávaro, who stepped down to run for Senate re-election in Mato Grosso. De Paula emphasized the agrobusiness sector’s importance to Brazil’s economy, accounting for 25% of GDP, employing over 28 million, and contributing to half of national exports. His appointment is part of Lula’s ministerial reform, with 20 ministers expected to leave office to contest the 2026 elections.

item.title: TCU investiga participação de servidores públicos em desfile de Lula
item.description: The Federal Court of Accounts (TCU) has opened an investigation into alleged improper use of public resources by the Planalto Palace in the samba school Acadêmicos de Niterói's parade, which honored President Lula at the Marques da Sapucaí. The court has ordered the Civil House and the Presidency's Secretariat of Communication to provide clarifications by March 24, under suspicion that public servants were used for political-electoral purposes.

item.title: Marina deixa ministério e fala em convites de partidos para eleições
item.description: Environment Minister Marina Silva announced her resignation from the Ministry of Environment and Climate Change, stating she has received invitations from multiple parties — including PT, PCdoB, PV, PSB, PSOL, PSD and others — to run in the 2026 elections, though she has not yet decided whether to run or for which office. Her departure comes amid disagreements within the federal government over oil exploration in the Equatorial Margin, a contentious issue involving five sedimentary basins in northern and northeastern Brazil.

item.title: O que muda com o aumento da licença-paternidade no Brasil
item.description: Brazil has approved a gradual extension of paternity leave, increasing it from 5 to 20 paid days by 2029, making it the longest in the Americas. The law, signed by President Lula, covers informal workers and adoption cases, with benefits suspended in cases of domestic violence. It aims to promote shared responsibility in childcare and reduce gender inequality in domestic labor.

item.title: Áudio: Chega ao Senado indicação de Jorge Messias para o STF
item.description: The Senate received on Wednesday (1st) the presidential message with the nomination of Jorge Messias as a minister of the Federal Supreme Court (STF), filling the vacancy left by the retirement of Luís Roberto Barroso. The nomination was formalized through MSF 7/2026.

item.title: Leite defende consenso após anúncio de Caiado ao Planalto
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite (PSD) advocated for political consensus after the PSD selected Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) as its presidential pre-candidate. Leite acknowledged policy and stylistic differences with Caiado but emphasized mutual respect and shared ground, arguing dialogue is key to overcoming Brazil’s polarization. The decision followed the withdrawal of Paraná Governor Ratinho Junior (PSD), with Leite and Caiado statistically tied in voter intention polls (3% vs 4%) per a March 2026 Quaest survey.

item.title: Secretária de Tarcísio defende projetos de longo prazo: ‘Nem no segundo mandato vamos ver’
item.description: São Paulo's Secretary of Environment, Infrastructure, and Logistics, Natália Resende, stated that the state government is prioritizing long-term projects — including the São Paulo-Campinas Intercity Train, the Santos-Guarujá Tunnel, and Sabesp’s investment plan — whose completion may extend beyond a potential second term of Governor Tarcísio de Freitas. In 2025, Sabesp invested R$15.2 billion in sanitation, a 120% increase from the previous year, marking a surge from annual investments of R$4–5 billion to R$15–20 billion.

item.title: “O Ciro é um destemperado”, diz Lula
item.description: Lula stated that former minister Ciro Gomes never reached the presidency due to the electorate's decision, not his own. He noted that Ciro dreamed of being a presidential candidate, ran multiple times, and lost — just as Lula himself has experienced in past elections.

item.title: “Se chegar na minha mão, com oito dias eu vou ler a mensagem na CCJ”, diz Otto Alencar sobre indicação de Messias ao STF
item.description: Senator Otto Alencar (PSD-BA), president of the Senate’s Constitution and Justice Committee (CCJ), said he will read the nomination message for Jorge Messias to the STF within eight days of receiving it and then schedule the confirmation hearing. He has not yet spoken with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre about a timeline but plans to meet with him next week. The presidential nomination was received by the Senate on Wednesday (1st) and awaits referral by Alcolumbre to the CCJ, after which Messias will face a hearing and a full Senate vote.

item.title: Governo inaugura alojamento estudantil do ITA Ceará
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and Education Minister Camilo Santana inaugurated the student housing at the new ITA Ceará campus in Fortaleza. The campus, developed through cooperation between the Education and Defense Ministries and the Ceará state government, will offer energy and systems engineering programs to decentralize high-level engineering education and expand access to quality higher education for students from all social backgrounds.

item.title: Marina deixa Ministério do Meio Ambiente sem definir se será candidata em São Paulo e se fica na Rede
item.description: Environment Minister Marina Silva resigned to run in the 2026 São Paulo elections, without yet deciding whether to seek a Senate seat or run as vice on Fernando Haddad's gubernatorial ticket. She said she remains available to 'fulfill her mission' and is awaiting a court decision in her internal party dispute with Heloísa Helena's faction in Rede Sustentabilidade. Her legacy includes reduced deforestation, restored environmental enforcement, and hosting COP30 in Brazil, despite failing to establish the National Climate Security Authority and losing the battle over oil exploration in the Amazon Mouth, where Ibama granted Petrobras a drilling permit off Amapá.

item.title: Quais os próximos passos após indicação de Jorge Messias para o STF
item.description: Jorge Messias' nomination to the STF by Lula requires Senate approval, involving a hearing in the CCJ and a secret vote in the plenary, needing an absolute majority of 41 senators. Despite historical support for presidential nominees, there are signs of resistance, with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre possibly delaying the process and opposition led by Flávio Bolsonaro aiming to reject the nomination. If approved, Messias will be published in the DOU and sworn in at the STF in a ceremony with the Three Powers.

item.title: No Ceará, Lula defende candidatura de Elmano e diz que Camilo é uma 'liderança que precisa ser usada em nível nacional'
item.description: Lula defended Elmano de Freitas' re-election bid in Ceará and stated that Camilo Santana, senator and former Education Minister, should be used as a national campaign asset due to his increased mobility after leaving office. He said Camilo has 'other flights to make' and could be called to run for office in the second half of the year if needed, without specifying the position. The remarks come amid a Datafolha poll showing Ciro Gomes leading gubernatorial intent in Ceará with 47%, followed by Elmano at 32%.

item.title: Planalto envia ao Senado mensagem de indicação de Jorge Messias para vaga no STF
item.description: The Planalto Palace sent the presidential message to the Senate nominating Attorney General Jorge Messias for a STF vacancy, four months after Lula’s formal announcement. Despite initial resistance from Senate President Davi Alcolumbre and bolsonarista senators, JOTA analysts report Messias has sufficient support for approval, aided by evangelical blocs and STF ministers like André Mendonça. Approval requires an absolute majority (41 votes) and is expected to pass by a narrow margin, akin to Paulo Gonet’s reappointment.
